fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Idaho Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Idaho - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity idaho, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to idaho, pharmacy technician compact idaho, idaho pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Idaho Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses (DOPL)
board.mailing_address:
Idaho Board of Pharmacy
1199 Shoreline Lane, Suite 303
Boise, ID 83702

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Idaho.

steps.title: 1. Create an Account on the IBOP Online Portal
steps.description: Go to the Idaho Board of Pharmacy Online Application Portal (ibopl.secure.force.com/OnlineApplication/) and click 'Register for an Account'. Complete the registration with your personal details, create a username and password to establish your online profile.

steps.title: 2. Submit the Initial Pharmacy Technician Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account, select 'New Application', and choose 'Pharmacy Technician Initial Application'. Complete all required sections of the online application form, including personal, educational (high school diploma or GED equivalent required), and employment information.

steps.title: 3. Provide Consent for Criminal Background Check & Upload Documents
steps.description: As part of the application process, you will provide consent for a fingerprint-based criminal history background check. Upload any required supporting documents such as your high school diploma or GED equivalent as prompted by the online application.

steps.title: 4. Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the $75.00 non-refundable application fee directly through the secure online portal using a credit/debit card. Note that a separate fee for the background check will be incurred later.

steps.title: 5. Await Board Review and Fingerprinting Instructions
steps.description: After submitting your complete application and fee, the Idaho Board of Pharmacy will review your submission. Once deemed complete, they will provide you with specific instructions for completing the fingerprint-based criminal history background check through the Idaho State Police and Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI).

steps.title: 6. Complete Fingerprinting and Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete your fingerprinting process. Ensure that the results of your criminal history background check are sent directly to the Idaho Board of Pharmacy for final review.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Idaho offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Idaho Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Idaho by submitting a new application to the Idaho Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Idaho-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 3 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Idaho pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Idaho have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Idaho. To work in Idaho, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Idaho?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Idaho by submitting a new application to the Idaho Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Idaho-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 3 Weeks.
